mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal expanding labor demand and income, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth coincides with negative 20d returns more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from above 7.5% to below 7.0% within three months indicates a tightening labor market, which historically precedes positive equity returns as the economy exits recession.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines to below 7.0% after being above 7.5% in the prior 3 months
failure_condition: The triggered signal produces a negative mean 20d return (t-test p<0.10) or the condition fires fewer than 5 times in the in-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal synchronized labor and real estate strength, amplifying the positive impact on aggregate demand and raising equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal's predictive power (rank IC) is not significantly greater than the sum of the individual signals (Hotelling's T2 test p>0.10), or the interaction term in a regression is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, further increases in the price level raise the probability of imminent monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods when the trigger is active, the average 20d forward return is non-negative (one-sided test p<0.05), or the mechanism yields false signals more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) reflects increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profits and signals sustainable expansion, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank IC for INDPRO changes against 5d forward returns is below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO prints coincide with negative returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 0.25% after a prolonged period at the zero lower bound signals the beginning of a tightening cycle, which reduces liquidity and raises discount rates, depressing equity prices over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% after being ≤0.25% for at least 6 months
failure_condition: The triggered signal produces an average 20d return greater than or equal to zero (p<0.10), or the condition occurs fewer than 3 times in the historical record.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low unemployment (UNRATE ≤6%) and rising retail sales (RSAFS month-over-month increase) indicates a consumer-driven expansion; when both conditions hold, the market tends to deliver positive returns as spending strength validates the labor market signal.
failure_condition: The interaction term in a regression of 20d returns on UNRATE and RSAFS changes is not significant (p>0.10), or the joint signal's hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) contracts quarter-over-quarter, the economy is in a recession regime where corporate earnings deteriorate, leading to negative quarterly forward returns for the S&P 500 as investors price in declining profits.
failure_condition: During quarters with negative GDP growth, the average 60d forward return is non-negative (one-sided test p<0.05), or the signal activates fewer than 4 times in the sample.
